title: Nanodrop dsDNA Quantification Protocol
author: Owen Butler
materials: Login to the computer, launch NanoEight Software.

steps.title: Select dsDNA for double stranded DNA...
steps.instruction: Select dsDNA for double stranded DNA (this is the most common sample type for our workflows). Figure 1. NanoDrop Eight software home page

steps.title: To clean the pedestals , gently...
steps.instruction: To clean the pedestals , gently wipe the pedestals with some DI water on a KimWipe. Ensure there is no water left behind from cleaning. Click "Ready" to continue.

steps.title: Select the desired pedestals for blanking...
steps.instruction: Select the desired pedestals for blanking (up to eight pedestals depending on the number of pedestals you will require). Figure 2. Selecting pedestals in the software

steps.title: Close the arm and hit run to initiate blanking
steps.instruction: Close the arm and hit run to initiate blanking. Figure 3. Running blank reads Ensure the blanks were accepted. Repeat the cleaning process by gently wiping the pedestals with some DI water on a KimWipe.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of sample to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of sample to the center of the pedestal. Bubbles or off-centered liquids may disrupt and distort the read. Record the order of samples in your ELN. Click the play button at the top right to measure up to 8 samples. Figure 4. Measuring samples

steps.title: End the experiment using the button in the top-right corner
steps.instruction: End the experiment using the button in the top-right corner. Select the desired file format(s) (e.g., .csv) and export the data to Excel. Upload the exported files to your preferred storage location, then rename the samples to reflect their corresponding identities. Figure 5. Ending run and exporting data

steps.title: Repeat the cleaning process once more,...
steps.instruction: Repeat the cleaning process once more, lower the pedestal arms, and close the software.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of NFW or...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of NFW or 1 µL Elution Buffer (depending on what your sample is in) to the center of the selected pedestals. Bubbles or off-centered liquids may disrupt and distort the read.

steps.title: If you have more than 8...
steps.instruction: If you have more than 8 samples to read, wipe the pedestals down with DI water on a KimWipe. Select the next column, load, and read your next samples. Repeat for however many samples you need to read, cleaning the pedestals in between each read of 8 samples. Do not perform another blank measurement between columns. All samples will have the same initial blank applied to their calculations.
